library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: SDL_CloseAsyncIO
identity.titleArabic: إغلاق free any allocated resources async I/O object

summary.actualTranslationArabic: إغلاق free any allocated resources async I/O object
summary.actualMeaningArabic: إغلاق free any allocated resources async I/O object.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى إغلاق free any allocated resources async I/O object داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic: القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه يعيد true عند النجاح وfalse عند الفشل؛ استخدم SDL_GetError لمعرفة السبب. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription: Close and free any allocated resources for an async I/O object.

signature.raw: bool SDL_CloseAsyncIO(SDL_AsyncIO *asyncio, bool flush, SDL_AsyncIOQueue *queue, void *userdata);

parameters.name: asyncio
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل asyncio من النوع SDL_AsyncIO * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
a pointer to an SDL_AsyncIO structure
to close.

parameters.name: flush
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل flush من النوع bool القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: true if data should sync to disk before the task completes.

parameters.name: queue
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل queue من النوع SDL_AsyncIOQueue * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
a queue to add the new SDL_AsyncIO
to.

parameters.name: userdata
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل userdata من النوع void * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
an app-defined pointer that will be provided with the task
results.

remarks:
Closing a file is also an asynchronous task! If a write
failure were to happen during the closing process, for example, the task
results will report it as usual.

Closing a file that has been written to does not guarantee the data
has made it to physical media; it may remain in the operating system's
file cache, for later writing to disk. This means that a
successfully-closed file can be lost if the system crashes or loses
power in this small window. To prevent this, call this function with the
 flush parameter set to true. This will make the operation
take longer, and perhaps increase system load in general, but a
successful result guarantees that the data has made it to physical
storage. Don't use this for temporary files, caches, and unimportant
data, and definitely use it for crucial irreplaceable files, like game
saves.

This function guarantees that the close will happen after any other
pending tasks to asyncio , so it's safe to open a file,
start several operations, close the file immediately, then check for all
results later. This function will not block until the tasks have
completed.

Once this function returns true, asyncio is no longer
valid, regardless of any future outcomes. Any completed tasks might
still contain this pointer in their SDL_AsyncIOOutcome data, in case the
app was using this value to track information, but it should not be used
again.

If this function returns false, the close wasn't started at all, and
it's safe to attempt to close again later.

An SDL_AsyncIOQueue must be
specified. The newly-created task will be added to it when it completes
its work.
details.returns: يعيد true عند النجاح وfalse عند الفشل؛ استخدم SDL_GetError لمعرفة السبب.
details.threadSafety:
It is safe to call this function from any thread, but two threads
should not attempt to close the same object.
